# Kapture

> Kapture is a free, open-source (MIT) all-in-one screenshot, OCR, and screen-recording tool for Linux X11 desktops (Kubuntu, Ubuntu, KDE Plasma). It is written in Python with PyQt5 and bundles region/window capture, automatic scrolling long screenshots, on-image annotation, Tesseract-based Chinese & English OCR, screen recording (MP4/GIF), pin-to-screen, and beautified export into a single tray app.

## What Kapture does

- **Region capture**: drag a box to capture any part of the screen.
- **Window capture**: click a window to capture it.
- **Scrolling long screenshot (auto)**: Kapture auto-scrolls a target window and stitches frames into one tall, seamless image — ideal for web pages, chat logs, code, and documents.
- **Scrolling long screenshot (manual)**: the user scrolls and Kapture stitches.
- **OCR text recognition**: Tesseract-powered, recognizes Simplified Chinese, Traditional Chinese, and English; result is copied to the clipboard. Layout modes and image enhancement improve accuracy.
- **Annotation**: rectangle, ellipse, arrow, line, pen, text, numbered steps, highlight, blur/mosaic (to hide sensitive info), magnifier, crop — with adjustable color and line width, undo, and clear.
- **Screen recording**: export to MP4 or GIF with adjustable frame rate.
- **Pin to screen**: keep a screenshot floating on top (drag to move, scroll to zoom, double-click to close).
- **Beautify export**: gradient background, rounded corners, drop shadow.
- **Workflow**: global keyboard shortcuts (written into KDE), system tray, single instance, configurable save folder and filename template, auto-copy/auto-save, history, accent themes, and a bilingual Chinese/English UI.

## Who it is for

Linux users who want a single tool for screenshots with OCR and scrolling long-screenshot support. Kapture is a practical Linux alternative to Snipaste, ShareX, and Flameshot, and one of the few Linux scrolling-screenshot tools with built-in OCR.

## Requirements

- An X11 session (`echo $XDG_SESSION_TYPE` returns `x11`). Wayland is not supported because simulated scrolling and full-screen grabbing are restricted there.
- System packages (apt): tesseract-ocr, tesseract-ocr-chi-sim, tesseract-ocr-chi-tra, fonts-noto-cjk, ffmpeg.
- Python packages (in a venv): PyQt5, mss, opencv-python-headless, numpy, pillow, pynput, pytesseract.

## Install and run

- Install: `git clone https://github.com/ycwei5/kapture.git && cd kapture && bash install.sh`
- Run: launch "Kapture" from the application menu, or run `./run.sh`.
- Direct actions: `./run.sh --region`, `--window`, `--scroll`, `--manual`, `--color`.

## Documentation

- [README (English)](https://github.com/ycwei5/kapture/blob/main/README.md): full feature overview, installation, usage, tips, and FAQ.
- [README (中文)](https://github.com/ycwei5/kapture/blob/main/README.zh-CN.md): Chinese documentation.
- [LICENSE](https://github.com/ycwei5/kapture/blob/main/LICENSE): MIT License.
- [kapture.py](https://github.com/ycwei5/kapture/blob/main/kapture.py): the entire application in a single readable Python file.

## Keywords

Linux screenshot tool, scrolling screenshot, long screenshot, full page screenshot, screenshot OCR, Tesseract OCR Linux, screen capture Ubuntu, Kubuntu KDE screenshot, screen recorder Linux, image annotation, blur sensitive info, pin screenshot, color picker, X11, PyQt5, Snipaste alternative Linux, ShareX alternative Linux, Flameshot alternative.
